1.description: Time Series Plot of Country and Global Idiosyncratic Return Variances (IVRET) Notes. This figure presents the time series plots of country and global idiosyncratic return variances (IVRET). (a) Time series plots of country IVRET for Germany, Japan, the United Kingdom, and the United States, which is value-weighted firm-level IVRET within each country. (b) Time series plot of global IVRET, which is value-weighted country IVRET. The shaded areas represent NBER recession periods.

2.description: Time Series Plot of Country and Global Idiosyncratic ROE Variances (IVROE) Notes. This figure presents the time series plots of country and global idiosyncratic ROE variances (IVROE). IVROE is transformed using the kernel method. (a) Time series plots of country IVROE for Germany, Japan, the United Kingdom, and the United States, which is value-weighted firm-level IVROE within each country. (b) Time series plot of global IVROE, which is value-weighted country IVROE. The shaded areas represent NBER recession periods.

3.description: Cyclicality of Global Idiosyncratic Variances Notes. This figure presents the time series plots of the rolling-window correlation between global idiosyncratic variances and GDP growth rates. In each quarter q , calculate the correlation over the quarters ( q − 19, q ) between global idiosyncratic variance with contemporaneous global GDP growth. GDP growth is the growth rate of trailing four-quarter real GDP compared with the same quarter of previous year. Global real GDP is constructed using nominal GDP and GDP deflator data for the OECD total from OECD. (a) Time series plot for global idiosyncratic return variance (IVRET). We also show the same plot for the fitted value of the regression on the state variables with GDP growth. (b) Time series plots for global idiosyncratic ROE variance (IVROE). The shaded areas represent NBER recession periods.
